title: asperula lilaciflora subsp. phrygia
intro: Asperula lilaciflora subsp. phrygia, also known as Phrygian Woodruff, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phrygian Woodruff include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems.
